Metro Council WA Local Government / Perth WA

About the council

A council for 85,000 people.

A metropolitan local government authority serving a population of approximately 85,000 residents across inner and middle suburbs of Perth. Responsible for local roads, parks, waste management, planning approvals, community facilities, libraries, and a growing suite of community development programs. Currently undergoing a digital transformation to modernise citizen services and internal operations.

85,000residents
12 suburbs / 4 wards
320staff
180 indoor / 140 outdoor
$95m
Annual revenue
1998
Year established

The council in numbers

What council does, at a glance.

A small team running a wide range of services for a growing community. These are the things council looks after.

Fact 1

85,000 residents across 12 suburbs

Fact 2

320 staff (180 indoor, 140 outdoor/operations)

Fact 3

Annual rates revenue: $62M

Fact 4

18 parks and reserves maintained

Fact 5

2 libraries, 3 community centres, 1 aquatic centre

Fact 6

Processing 2,400+ planning applications per year

Leadership team

The directorate.

Five directors and a chief executive. Council policy is set by elected members; the directorate is responsible for delivering it.

A
Aiden Cross
Director of Digital & Information Services

F
Farah Haddad
Director of Planning & Development

H
Helen Marchetti
Chief Executive Officer

M
Margaret Chen
Director of Corporate Services & Finance

Want to know what council is working on?

Three consultations are open right now and the council meeting calendar is published a year in advance. Council decisions are open to the public.

Your council

Metro Council WA

Delivering local government services to 85,000 residents across 12 suburbs in inner and middle Perth.

Metro Council WA acknowledges the Whadjuk people of the Noongar nation as the Traditional Custodians of the lands on which we work, and we pay our respects to Elders past and present.

A fictional council, made for a Curtin University internship simulation.
